title_en: Dragonlance: War Wizard Is the Next Novel in the Legendary Fantasy Series From Margaret Weis and Tracy Hickman — and We’ve Got an Exclusive Excerpt
summary_en: Dragonlance: War Wizard is the next novel in the legendary fantasy series from Margaret Weis and Tracy Hickman — and we've got an exclusive excerpt.
body_en:
Dragonlance is, for me, a formative fantasy series. As a young teen, the orcs and elves and hobbits of Tolkien's Middle-earth passed me by. Instead, I was entranced by Margaret Weis and Tracy Hickman’s Dragons of Autumn Twilight, first published in 1984. Oblivious to the games of Dungeon & Dragons upon which the novel was based, I fell in love with the book at face value. For years, my school bag always had at least one book in The Dragonlance Chronicles or the Dragonlance Legends trilogies inside, ready for yet another read through.
40(!) years later, Weis and Hickman have a new Dragonlance novel, called War Wizard, out in August. So I’m thrilled to be able to introduce this exclusive excerpt on IGN.
War Wizard is set before the original Dragonlance Chronicles and tells the story of the early years of future legends Huma Dragonbane and Magius during the Third Dragon War. This coming-of-age prequel explores how two young heroes rise amid the growing threat of dragons, magic, and war.
Here’s the official blurb:
“This scene was a lot of fun to write,” Weis told IGN, setting the scene for the exclusive excerpt, below. “Doppelgangers are among the deadliest monsters in D&D. They make excellent assassins because they acquire a person’s surface memories, as well as the physical features. This enables the shapeshifter to assume the aspect of a friend or loved one, thus enabling the monster to get close to their victim. To make matters worse, they are magic resistant, as Magius sadly discovers.”
Excerpt from Dragonlance: War Wizard by Margaret Weis and Tracy Hickman (Publishing August 4, 2026; Random House Worlds):
